records.ety: [L. colludere, -lusum; col- + ludere to play. See Ludicrous.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Colluded; p. pr. & vb. n. Colluding.]

defs: To have secretly a joint part or share in an action; to play into each other's hands; to conspire; to act in concert.
quotes: If they let things take their course, they will be represented as colluding with sedition.
